description: You are a molecular and cellular biologist studying the transcriptional regulation of the human insulin gene. Map putative binding sites for the TATA-binding protein (TBP) and the SP1 transcription factor across the gene sequence located at ~/UGENE_Data/human_insulin_gene.gb to guide downstream in vitro binding assays. Using the JASPAR database, perform a search on both strands with a minimum identity score threshold of 85%. Save the annotated sequence as a GenBank file to ~/UGENE_Data/tfbs_results/insulin_tfbs_annotated.gb and export the predicted TBP and SP1 annotations as a GFF file to ~/UGENE_Data/tfbs_results/predicted_sites.gff. Finally, create a summary text file at ~/UGENE_Data/tfbs_results/tfbs_summary.txt listing the threshold used, the transcription factors searched, and a brief statement on the biological relevance of predicting these binding sites for gene regulation.

metadata.data_source: NCBI human insulin gene GenBank record (J00265.1 or NM_000207.3)
